skill.name: web-artifacts-builder
skill.description: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, multiple components, and professional UI styling.
skill.summary: Build complex web artifacts with React, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui - bundle to single HTML for Claude Code
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 48 potential issues, but all are false positives or benign patterns. The skill uses legitimate build tooling (pnpm, Vite, Parcel) for creating React projects with Tailwind CSS. Network URLs point to official documentation (shadcn/ui). Shell commands and filesystem operations are standard development scripts with hardcoded arguments. No malicious intent detected.

high_findings.title: Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm References
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns in LICENSE.txt. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the Apache License 2.0 contains standard legal text about cryptographic patents. No actual cryptographic code exists.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: LICENSE.txt is a text file containing Apache 2.0 license text. References to cryptography are in legal boilerplate about patent grants, not actual code implementation.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Build Scripts
medium_findings.description: Scripts execute shell commands (pnpm, node, sed, tar). All commands use hardcoded arguments with no user input injection. Standard build tooling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Commands like 'node -v', 'pnpm install', 'sed' are legitimate development tools. Arguments are hardcoded, no command injection vector exists.

medium_findings.title: Node.js Filesystem Operations
medium_findings.description: Scripts use Node.js fs module to read/write config files (tsconfig.json). Standard project setup pattern.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: fs.readFileSync and fs.writeFileSync modify tsconfig.json with project paths. This is standard tooling behavior, not malicious file access.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: URLs point to official shadcn/ui documentation (ui.shadcn.com) and Apache license (apache.org). Legitimate references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are to official project documentation and license references. No external data transmission or suspicious network behavior.

content.value_statement: Creating complex web artifacts in Claude Code requires setting up a React project, installing dependencies, and bundling to a single HTML file. This skill automates the entire workflow so you can focus on building the artifact.
actual_capabilities: Initialize a new React + Vite project with TypeScript
Install and configure Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui theming
Configure path aliases for clean imports
Bundle the React app into a single self-contained HTML file
Generate artifacts ready for direct use in Claude conversations
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or higher on the system
Creates project files locally - does not run in-browser
Bundle size may be large for very complex artifacts

use_cases.title: Create Interactive Data Visualizations
use_cases.description: Build complex charts, graphs, and data dashboards using React and shadcn/ui components. Bundle to HTML artifact for sharing.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts and developers who need interactive visualizations

use_cases.title: Build Multi-Form Workflows
use_cases.description: Create multi-step forms with state management, validation, and professional UI using React and Tailwind CSS.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and developers building user workflows

use_cases.title: Develop Design System Showcases
use_cases.description: Build comprehensive component showcases and design system documentation with multiple variants.
use_cases.target_user: Designers and frontend developers showcasing UI libraries

prompt_templates.title: Initialize New Artifact Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-artifacts-builder skill to initialize a new React project called 'my-dashboard'. Set up Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new complex web artifact from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Build Interactive Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the web-artifacts-builder skill, create a data dashboard with interactive charts, filter controls, and a data table using shadcn/ui components.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a dashboard with multiple interactive components

prompt_templates.title: Bundle and Share Artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bundle-artifact.sh script to bundle the current React project into a single HTML file that I can share as an artifact.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finishing development and preparing artifact for sharing

prompt_templates.title: Add New Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a dialog component and a form with validation using react-hook-form and zod to the current project. Configure the form to handle user input.
prompt_templates.scenario: Extending an existing artifact with new functionality

output_examples.input: Create a new React artifact project called 'analytics-dashboard'
output_examples.output: A fully configured React + Vite project with Tailwind CSS and 40+ shadcn/ui components ready for development

output_examples.input: Bundle the current project to HTML
output_examples.output: A single bundle.html file containing all JavaScript, CSS, and dependencies - ready to paste directly into Claude as an artifact

best_practices: Use path aliases (@/) for cleaner imports across components
Leverage shadcn/ui components for consistent, accessible UI
Test the artifact in browser before bundling for final sharing
Keep component logic simple - complex state may bloat the bundle
anti_patterns: Avoid installing additional npm packages after initial setup - each adds to bundle size
Do not skip the browser testing step - some React features behave differently
Avoid excessive external dependencies that cannot be bundled into single HTML

faq.question: What is the minimum Node.js version required?
faq.answer: Node.js 18 or higher is required. The skill auto-detects your Node version and adjusts Vite compatibility.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with npm or yarn instead of pnpm?
faq.answer: The skill currently requires pnpm. You can modify the scripts to use npm or yarn if needed.

faq.question: How large can the bundled HTML file be?
faq.answer: There is no strict limit, but artifacts over 2MB may be slow to load in Claude. Complex projects with many components work best.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports claude, codex, and claude-code tools.

faq.question: Can I add custom fonts to the artifact?
faq.answer: Yes, add font imports to index.css before bundling. Note that external fonts increase bundle size.

faq.question: How do I test the artifact before sharing?
faq.answer: Open bundle.html directly in your browser after running the bundle script to verify it works correctly.
